data.body: Most of us interact with the world without thinking about how. A big part of this ability is sensory feedback about body movement, but because of pathway delays, we can't rely only on feedback. Instead, we integrate feedback with motor signals ("corollary discharge") into an estimated body state. 2/
data.author: did:plc:arxyl566oltqhyunrjm7dcrc

data.body: CD and FB signals in area 2 also seem to be orthogonal to each other in the full neural state space. This echos similar findings in other cortical areas, showing that, for example, motor preparation and execution lie in orthogonal neural subspaces. 7/

data.body:
[라프텔 새 에피소드 알림]
<힘내라! 나카무라 군!!>
3화 "혹시? 이건 사랑!?"

오늘 미술 시간은 스케치 수업. 전혀 의욕이 없던 나카무라였지만, 모델이 히로세로 선정된 순간, 손이 안 보일 정도로 빠르게 연필을 움직였다. 눈치 보지 않고 히로세를 관찰할 수 있는 공간에서, 히로세의 속눈썹부터 입술까지 꼼꼼하게 눈에 새겨두는 나카무라. '히로세 스케치는 누구보다도 자신 있지' 의기양양하던 나카무라는, 옆자리에 앉은 카와무라 히후미의 스케치를 보게 되는데...

data.body:
A tangential question. To quote CNBC:

“The Company will initially seek to acquire high-performance, low-latency AI compute hardware…”

Did “compute” become an adjective while I wasn’t looking? Wouldn’t “computing” or “computational” be better? Or “computer” (which became an adjective long ago)?
